description: §61.2/§61.10 — an `<endpoint>` arm with a BARE body containing TWO OR MORE statements (a bare `seq` then the result object) fires E-ENDPOINT-MULTI-STATEMENT-ARM. An arm body is lowered as a single value-expression the compiler envelopes (§61.5), so a 2+-statement body has no single-expression lowering. The clean named diagnostic REPLACES the generic E-CODEGEN-INVALID-LOGIC gate.
rationale: §61.10: a multi-statement bare-body arm is a future-wave gap (the arm body is one value-expression). Codegen detects the 2+-statement body and fires E-ENDPOINT-MULTI-STATEMENT-ARM at the arm (W4), NOT the cryptic E-CODEGEN-INVALID-LOGIC.
